description: Those who come after, for whom a path is cleared and a legacy is left. They are the future beings who will stand on the foundation created by the present.
rationale: This word echoes its elder 'thalume' (ancestor) through the flowing 'lum' that carries lineage forward like a river carrying life. But where 'thalume' begins with reverent breath and ends in grounding, 'lumira' starts with that ancestral flow and brightens into 'mi.ra'—the personal spark rising into future radiance. The progression from flowing heritage through individual presence to bright possibility captures how descendants are both continuations and new beginnings. They carry the stream forward while adding their own light to the flow.
